---
title: "Wildcloud to Movebank Workflow"
author: "Edward Hurme"
date: "Updated: 2026-05-10"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
---

# Overview

This guide describes how to take raw tag data from the WildCloud platform and
upload it to Movebank. The pipeline is implemented in
`R/wildcloud_to_movebank.R` (github.com/ehurme/SigfoxTagPrep).

**Tag models and firmware supported:**

| Model | Firmware | Years active | VeDBA type |
|---|---|---|---|
| TinyFoxBatt | V13, V13P, V14P, TV1, BBV6, BBX5 | 2022–2024 | Cumulative daily total |
| NanoFox | 30Days, DailyVeDBA, 10Day, BBX5 | 2025 | Windowed sum (36 min) |
| NanoFox | 30DaysFineScalePressure (FSP) | 2026 | Windowed sum (36 min) |
| TinyFoxTwo | TINYFOXTWO BLACKBIRDS V | 2024–2026 | Windowed sum (60 min) |

---

# 1. Organize the Capture Sheet

The capture sheet is a CSV or Excel file with one row per tag deployment.
Required columns (any recognized spelling variant works — see auto-detection
rules in `wildcloud_to_movebank()`):

| Field | Example | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Tag ID | `120EBBA` | WildCloud device ID |
| Species / Animal taxon | `Nyctalus leisleri` | Genus + species, or full taxon string |
| Deployment date | `2025-08-14` | ISO format preferred |
| Deployment time | `21:30:00` | HMS; defaults to noon if missing |
| Capture latitude | `47.6123` | Decimal degrees WGS84 |
| Capture longitude | `9.0456` | Decimal degrees WGS84 |

**Recommended additional columns:**

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| Ring number / Ring ID | Used as the Movebank `animal.ring.id` and as `Animal.ID` when present |
| PIT tag ID | Takes priority over ring number for `Animal.ID` |
| Animal mass (g) | Body mass at deployment |
| Animal sex | M / F |
| Life stage | adult / juvenile |
| Forearm length (mm) | |
| Tag model | e.g. `NanoFox`, `TinyFoxBatt` |
| Firmware version | e.g. `30Days`, `30DaysFineScalePressure` |

**Animal ID assignment** (automatic, in priority order):

1. PIT tag ID (most stable — survives recapture and re-tagging)
2. Ring / band number
3. `Animal_ID` column if present
4. Structured fallback: `{Gspp}{YY}_{location}_{TagID}` — e.g. `Nlei25_Swiss_120EBBA`

Pass `location_abbr = "Swiss"` (or similar 3–6 character code) to get
meaningful fallback IDs. Without it the fallback uses `Unk`.

---

# 2. Download Data from WildCloud

1. Log in to wildcloud.icarus.mpg.de
2. Select your device group / project
3. Set the date range and export as CSV

**How many records to download:**
Tags can send a maximum of ~300–400 messages over their lifetime. A safe upper
bound is `n_tags × 400`. The WildCloud website may crash on very large exports
— if so, split the download by tag ID and save all CSVs into the same folder.
The R function merges them automatically.

**WildCloud CSV columns vary by firmware version.** The function
`harmonize_wc_columns()` normalizes all known variants to canonical names
before processing, so you do not need to rename columns manually.

---

# 3. Convert to Movebank Format in R

Source the function file, then call one of two functions depending on whether
this is a **new study** or an **update to an existing study**.

```r
source("R/wildcloud_to_movebank.R")
```

## 3a. New study — `wildcloud_to_movebank()`

Use this for the **first upload** of a study, or when you want to regenerate
all files from scratch without querying Movebank.

```r
result <- wildcloud_to_movebank(
  wc_path               = "path/to/wildcloud/csvs/",   # folder of WildCloud exports
  # movebank_csv_path   = "path/to/single_export.csv", # alternative: one merged CSV
  animals_path          = "path/to/capture_sheet.xlsx",
  output_dir            = "path/to/movebank_output/",
  movebank_project_name = "ICARUS Bats. Nyctalus leisleri. Switzerland",
  location_abbr         = "Swiss"                       # used in fallback Animal.IDs
)
```

**Key optional arguments:**

| Argument | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `location_abbr` | `"Unk"` | Short code embedded in fallback Animal.IDs |
| `species` | auto-detected | Override species string for all rows |
| `life_stage` | auto-detected | Override life stage for all rows |
| `animal_id_col` | auto-detected | Force a specific column as Animal.ID |
| `clean_lat_range` | `NULL` | Drop fixes outside `c(min_lat, max_lat)` |
| `clean_lon_range` | `NULL` | Drop fixes outside `c(min_lon, max_lon)` |
| `force_tag_model_family` | auto | Override tag model (e.g. `"NanoFox"`) |
| `force_firmware_version` | auto | Override firmware (e.g. `"30Days"`) |
| `force_vedba_count` | auto | Override the burst count used for VeDBA avg |
| `correct_drift` | `FALSE` | Apply clock-drift correction (requires `lme4`) |
| `programmed_interval` | `NULL` | Tag transmission interval in seconds (for drift) |
| `sampling_config` | built-in | Custom firmware sampling table |

**What the function does internally:**

1. Reads the capture sheet and resolves Animal IDs (PIT > ring > fallback)
2. Reads all WildCloud CSVs, normalizes column names across firmware versions
3. Expands the wide per-transmission schema into long Movebank-style rows:
   - **Location row** — timestamp + lat/lon (one per transmission)
   - **VeDBA rows** — 5 × 36-min windows per transmission (NanoFox), or cumulative daily total (TinyFoxBatt)
   - **Temperature rows** — 5 × 36-min average bins
   - **Pressure row** — minimum of last 3 hrs (or 5 instantaneous readings for FSP)
   - **Min/max temperature row** — 3-hr summary
4. Optionally applies clock-drift correction at transmission level
5. Joins firmware sampling metadata (vedba_count, burst_rate_hz, etc.)
6. Computes `deploy.on.date` / `deploy.off.date` for the deployment table
7. Writes per-project CSVs to `output_dir/Projects/{project_name}/`

**Output files written:**

| File | Movebank sensor type | Content |
|---|---|---|
| `data.csv` | `sigfox-geolocation` | Locations with Sigfox metadata |
| `dataVeDBA.csv` | `acceleration` | VeDBA sum + avg, firmware metadata |
| `dataBar.csv` | `barometer` | Minimum barometric pressure |
| `dataTemp.csv` | `accessory-measurements` | Average temperature bins |
| `dataMinTempText.csv` | `Derived` | Min temp as categorical range label (30Days) |
| `dataMinMaxTemp.csv` | `accessory-measurements` | Numeric min + max temp (FSP firmware) |
| `deployment.csv` | — | One row per deployment; upload first |

**Inspecting the result:**

```r
# All processed sensor data (before splitting into per-type tables)
head(result$movebank_df)

# Deployment table
result$deployment_data

# Firmware sampling config used
result$sampling_config

# Drift model (if correct_drift = TRUE)
drift_model <- attr(result, "drift_model")
summary(drift_model)
```

### Clock-drift correction

Tags without a real-time clock accumulate timing error over the deployment.
Set `correct_drift = TRUE` to model and remove this per-tag drift. The
correction requires the `lme4` package.

```r
source("R/correct_time_drift.R")   # must be sourced separately

result <- wildcloud_to_movebank(
  wc_path             = "path/to/wildcloud/csvs/",
  animals_path        = "path/to/capture_sheet.xlsx",
  output_dir          = "path/to/movebank_output/",
  movebank_project_name = "ICARUS Bats. Nyctalus leisleri. Switzerland",
  correct_drift       = TRUE,
  programmed_interval = 3 * 3600,          # 3-hour schedule in seconds
  drift_temp_col      = "temperature [°C]",# include temperature as drift predictor
  drift_diagnostics   = TRUE               # saves diagnostic plots to output_dir
)
```

---

## 3b. Updating an existing study — `update_wildcloud_movebank()`

Use this when the study **already exists on Movebank** and you are adding new
data from a later field season or download batch. The function queries Movebank
to find which tags already have deployments, then:

- **Always** writes all data files for the new WildCloud export
- **Only writes `deployment.csv`** if new (un-deployed) tags are found; in
  that case it covers all animals in the capture sheet (whole-study upload)
- **Stamps every data file** with a `run_label` suffix so multiple uploads
  accumulate in the same folder without overwriting each other

Requires Movebank credentials stored via `move2::movebank_store_credentials()`.

```r
library(move2)
# Run once to store credentials:
# movebank_store_credentials(username = "your_username", password = "your_password")

result <- update_wildcloud_movebank(
  study_id              = 123456789,        # Movebank study ID (from the study URL)
  wc_path               = "path/to/new_wildcloud_csvs/",
  animals_path          = "path/to/capture_sheet.xlsx",
  output_dir            = "path/to/movebank_output/",
  movebank_project_name = "ICARUS Bats. Nyctalus leisleri. Switzerland",
  run_label             = "2026-05-10",     # appended to all data file names
  location_abbr         = "Swiss"
)
```

**What the function does:**

1. Downloads the existing deployment table from Movebank (`study_id`)
2. Runs the full `wildcloud_to_movebank()` pipeline on the new data
3. Renames all data files: `data_2026-05-10.csv`, `dataVeDBA_2026-05-10.csv`, etc.
4. Compares tag IDs in the new export against already-deployed tags:
   - **No new tags** → removes `deployment.csv`, writes `NO_DEPLOYMENT_UPLOAD_NEEDED.txt`
   - **New tags found** → keeps `deployment.csv` (whole study), writes `DEPLOYMENT_UPLOAD_REQUIRED.txt` listing the new tags and upload order

**`run_label`** defaults to today's date (`"2026-05-10"`). You can pass any
alphanumeric string, e.g. `"spring2026"` or `"2026-05-10_navarre"`.

**Inspecting the update assessment:**

```r
result$update_assessment$tags_needing_deployment   # new tags requiring deployment entries
result$update_assessment$tags_already_deployed     # tags already in Movebank
result$update_assessment$deployment_needed         # TRUE / FALSE
result$update_assessment$existing_deployments      # full Movebank deployment table
```

---

# 4. Quick Diagnostic Look at Raw WildCloud Data — `wildcloud_quicklook()`

Before converting to Movebank format it is useful to visually check what the
raw WildCloud export looks like: which tags are transmitting, whether locations
look sensible, and what the VeDBA and temperature traces look like. The
`wildcloud_quicklook()` function in `R/wildcloud_quicklook.R` does this
directly from the WildCloud CSV without needing a capture sheet.

```r
source("R/wildcloud_quicklook.R")

out <- wildcloud_quicklook(
  "path/to/wildcloud_export.csv",   # file path OR an already-loaded data.frame
  start_time      = "2026-04-09",   # optional date/time filter
  end_time        = NULL,
  plot_dir        = "path/to/plots/"  # omit to display without saving
)
```

**What it produces:**

| Plot | Name in `out$plots` | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Daily distance | `daily_distance` | Total step distance per tag per day |
| Daily VeDBA | `daily_vedba` | Mean VeDBA sum per tag per day |
| Speed over time | `speed_over_time` | Step speed (m/s), faceted per tag |
| Transmission timeline | `transmission_timeline` | All transmissions; blue = valid fix, red = flagged |
| Raw VeDBA (time-corrected) | `raw_vedba` | VeDBA bins plotted at their measurement time |
| Altitude (ISA) | `raw_altitude` | Pressure converted to altitude via ISA formula |
| Temperature | `raw_temperature` | Min + max 3-hr temperature |
| Map | `map` | Track lines + points over country outlines |

Night periods (sunset to sunrise) are shaded grey in all time-series plots,
computed from the median tag location for each device.

**Key optional arguments:**

| Argument | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `start_time` / `end_time` | `NULL` | Subset to a date or datetime range |
| `min_quality` | `NULL` | Minimum LQI: `"Poor"`, `"Average"`, `"Good"`, `"Excellent"` |
| `max_radius` | `NULL` | Drop locations with position radius > this (metres) |
| `gap_hours` | `3` | Hours between fixes that breaks a track segment in line plots |
| `plot_daily` | `TRUE` | Show daily distance and VeDBA summary plots |
| `plot_raw` | `TRUE` | Show per-fix speed, VeDBA, altitude, and timeline plots |
| `plot_temp` | `TRUE` | Show temperature panel |
| `free_y_raw` | `TRUE` | Each tag gets its own y-axis in faceted plots |
| `facets_per_page` | `20` | Max tags per page; automatically paginates beyond this |
| `plot_dir` | `NULL` | If set, saves all plots as PNGs to this directory |

**Accessing results:**

```r
# Data table with computed columns (step_dist_m, speed_ms, vedba_sum, coord_ok, ...)
head(out$data)

# Per-device summary (n_fixes, mean speed, max VeDBA, ...)
out$summary

# Individual plots
print(out$plots$map)
print(out$plots$daily_distance)

# If > 20 devices, raw plots are paginated — access by page
print(out$plots$speed_over_time_p1)
print(out$plots$speed_over_time_p2)
```

**Coordinate filtering:** Locations outside 35–72 °N / 15 °W–40 °E are
automatically flagged (`coord_ok = FALSE`) and excluded from distance and speed
calculations but retained in the data. Pass `clean_lat_range` /
`clean_lon_range` to `wildcloud_to_movebank()` later to hard-drop these rows
from the Movebank output.

---

# 6. Upload Files to Movebank

Log in to movebank.org and navigate to your study.

**Upload order matters:**

1. **`deployment.csv`** — must be uploaded first, before any data files.
   Skip this file on update runs where no new tags were found
   (`NO_DEPLOYMENT_UPLOAD_NEEDED.txt` will be present).
2. **`data.csv`** — GPS / Sigfox locations
3. **`dataVeDBA.csv`** — acceleration / VeDBA
4. **`dataBar.csv`** — barometric pressure
5. **`dataTemp.csv`** — temperature (36-min bins)
6. **`dataMinTempText.csv`** — categorical min temperature (30Days firmware)
7. **`dataMinMaxTemp.csv`** — numeric min + max temperature (FSP firmware)

Not all files will exist for every study — only upload files that are present
in the project folder.

**On update runs**, upload the `run_label`-stamped files:
e.g. `data_2026-05-10.csv`, `dataVeDBA_2026-05-10.csv`, etc.

---

# 7. Check the Upload in R

After uploading, verify the data arrived correctly using the `move2` package.

```r
library(move2)

# Authenticate (run once)
# movebank_store_credentials(username = "your_username", password = "your_password")

# List your studies
movebank_list_studies()

# Download and inspect
bats <- movebank_download_study(study_id = 123456789)
head(bats)
mt_n_tracks(bats)

# Check deployment coverage
deps <- movebank_retrieve("deployment", study_id = 123456789)
deps[, c("tag_local_identifier", "deploy_on_date", "deploy_off_date")]

# Quick map
library(mapview)
mapview(bats)
```
